narration: I didn't answer it. I did something dumber. At five to three I took the master key and rode up to four — to be standing at that door when the call happens. The corridor up there feels longer than the building. Room 408 is painted shut, sealed over like a scar. At three exactly, I heard it. Not ringing. Dialing. A rotary phone turning, patiently, inside a room with no line, no power, no guest. Under the door, a light passed. Slow. Searching. And four floors down, in my empty lobby, the front desk began to ring. I ran. The elevator opened on the lobby — and the receiver was already off the hook.

shots.prompt: Deniz Aksoy (burgundy waistcoat, brass key on a leather fob in his fist) steps into the vintage elevator; the brass accordion-cage doors fold shut across his tense face; above, the floor-indicator arrow begins to climb from the lobby mark. Ends on: the arrow sweeping past the third-floor mark.

Deniz Aksoy (burgundy waistcoat) walks slowly down the long fourth-floor corridor, wall sconces flickering as he passes each one, the patterned carpet stretching in deep perspective toward the darker sealed door at the far end with its old room-service tray. Ends on: him stopping a few steps short of the door, chest rising and falling.

Deniz Aksoy (burgundy waistcoat) leans his ear close to the sealed door of room 408, breath held; the sconce beside him dies; in the gap under the door a slow band of warm light sweeps past from inside, and he jerks back a step. Ends on: the band of light returning and STOPPING at the gap, as if it noticed him.

Close on the door of room 408: the faded 'Do Not Disturb' card begins to sway gently on the handle though the air is dead still; the brass peephole glints faintly warm from within; a hairline crack creeps along the old paint sealing the frame. Ends on: the peephole going suddenly dark.

Deniz Aksoy (burgundy waistcoat) sprints back down the long corridor toward camera, sconces dying one by one close behind him as darkness swallows the corridor's far end; ahead the elevator's cage doors already stand open, glowing amber, waiting. Ends on: the cage doors folding shut across his terrified face.

Deniz Aksoy (burgundy waistcoat) bursts out into the lamplit lobby and stops dead: on the reception counter the switchboard receiver lies OFF the hook across the open logbook, its coiled cord swinging slowly, a fresh line of wet ink glistening on the page. Ends on: Deniz frozen in the middle of the vast dark lobby, staring at the desk.
